F. 5/1/2014-FÂŁTF7R;SF] F^FT FRF, FFFF FRRT 3003GI/2016 (1) -- 1 of 2 -- 2 T H E G A Z E T T E O F I N D I A : E X T R A O R D I N A R Y [PART IIâ€”SEC. 3(ii)] MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 13th June, 2016 S.O. 2097(E).â€“ In exercise of the powers conferred by section 13 of the Territorial Waters, Continental Shelf, Exclusive Economic Zone and other Maritime Zones Act,1976 read with paragraph 2 of Part II of the Notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Home Affairs number S.O.671(E), dated the 27th August, 1981, published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (ii), the Central Government hereby directs that the police stations, namely, Navibandar Coastal Police Station, District Porbandar (Gujarat), Yellow Gate Police Station, Mumbai (Maharashtra and Daman and Diu), Harbour Coastal Security Police Station, Harbour, Mormugao, District South Goa (Goa), Mangalore Coastal Security Police Station, District Dakshina Kannada (Karnataka), Fort Kochi Coastal Police Station, Kochi (Kerala and Lakshadweep), B5 Harbour Police Station, Chennai (Tamil Nadu and Puducherry), Gilakaladindi, Machilipatnam, District Krishna (Andhra Pradesh), Paradeep Marine Police Station, District Jagatsinghpur (Odisha), Nayachar Coastal Police Station, District Purba Medinipur (West Bengal), and Central Crimes Station, Port Blair (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) shall be the Police Stations for the purpose of the investigating the offence committed by any person under any of the provisions of any Act as so extended to the exclusive economic zone under sub-section (7) of Section 7 of the said Act. [F. No. 5/1/2014-Coastal Security] PRADEEP GUPTA, Jt. Secy. Uploaded by Dte. of Printing at Government of India Press, Ring Road, Mayapuri, New Delhi-110064 and Published by the Controller of Publications, Delhi-110054. -- 2 of 2 --
